title: Two Sum & Three Sum
description: Classic array search problems — from brute force to optimized to triplets

parts.title: Two Sum — Brute Force
parts.description: Implement twoSum(nums, target) that returns the indices of two numbers adding up to target. Any working approach is fine.
activeTests: no solution returns undefined or empty

scaffold.python:
"""
Two Sum

Given a list of integers `nums` and an integer `target`,
return the indices of the two numbers that add up to `target`.

You may assume each input has exactly one solution,
and you may not use the same element twice.
"""


def two_sum(nums: list[int], target: int) -> list[int]:

parts.title: Two Sum — Optimized
parts.description: Optimize your twoSum to handle large inputs efficiently. Hint: use a hash map for O(n) time.
activeTests: no solution returns undefined or empty

parts.title: Three Sum
parts.description: Implement threeSum(nums, target) that returns all unique triplets [i, j, k] where nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] === target. No duplicate triplets allowed.
activeTests: no solution returns undefined or empty
